keep.reason: Most appropriate naming - 'resource' is generic and covers all EFS resources. All 3 rules check identical condition (Encrypted equals 'true').

remove.reason: EXACT DUPLICATE - All 3 rules check identical condition (Encrypted equals 'true'). aws.efs.resource.encryption_at_rest_enabled has better naming.
EXACT DUPLICATE - All 3 rules check identical condition (Encrypted equals 'true'). aws.efs.resource.encryption_at_rest_enabled has better naming.

keep.reason: More appropriate naming - 'resource' is generic. Both rules check identical condition (Status equals ENABLED).

remove.reason: EXACT DUPLICATE - Both check identical condition (Status equals ENABLED). aws.efs.resource.backup_enabled has better naming.

keep.reason: More descriptive naming - 'private_network_only' is clearer than 'not_publicly_accessible'. Both rules check identical conditions (SubnetId exists AND VpcId exists).

remove.reason: EXACT DUPLICATE - Both check identical conditions (SubnetId exists AND VpcId exists). aws.efs.filesystem.private_network_only_configured has better naming.

recommendations.description: Merge 3 duplicate rules checking Encrypted equals 'true'. Keep resource.encryption_at_rest_enabled (best naming).
recommendations.impact: Reduces duplicate rules from 3 to 1, preserves compliance coverage (43 standards from encryption.at_rest_enabled)

recommendations.description: Merge 2 duplicate rules checking Status equals ENABLED. Keep resource.backup_enabled.
Merge 2 duplicate rules checking SubnetId exists AND VpcId exists. Keep filesystem.private_network_only_configured.
Verify if Encrypted returns boolean true or string 'true'. Adjust values accordingly.
Enhance validation - currently only checks if KmsKeyId exists, but should verify KMS key policy follows least privilege. Need to check actual KMS key policy, not just that key exists.
recommendations.impact: Improves validation accuracy - ensures KMS key policy is actually least privilege

recommendations.description: Fix wrong field/concept - FileSystemProtection is about deletion protection, not snapshots. EFS doesn't have traditional snapshots - it uses backup policies. Should check backup policy instead.
recommendations.impact: Fixes wrong validation - checks correct backup mechanism

recommendations.description: Enhance validation - currently checks NumberOfMountTargets >= 1, but that doesn't verify Multi-AZ. Should check that mount targets exist in multiple availability zones.
recommendations.impact: Improves validation accuracy - ensures actual Multi-AZ configuration

recommendations.description: Enhance validation - currently only checks if SubnetId and VpcId exist, but doesn't verify subnet is private. Should check subnet configuration (e.g., mapPublicIpOnLaunch=false) or verify subnet is in private subnet range.
recommendations.impact: Improves validation accuracy - ensures subnet is actually private

validation_issues: 3 duplicate encryption rules checking Encrypted equals 'true'
2 duplicate backup rules checking Status equals ENABLED
2 duplicate private network rules checking SubnetId/VpcId exist
4 rules use string 'true' but API may return boolean
1 rule checks wrong field (FileSystemProtection for snapshots)
1 rule has insufficient Multi-AZ validation (checks mount target count, not multiple AZs)
2 rules have insufficient private network validation (don't verify subnet is private)

api_structure.Encrypted: May return boolean true or string 'true' - needs verification
api_structure.NumberOfMountTargets: Count of mount targets, doesn't indicate Multi-AZ
api_structure.SubnetId/VpcId: Always exist for mount targets, doesn't indicate private subnet

issues: 7 duplicate rules (3 encryption, 2 backup, 2 private network)
4 rules have value type issues (string vs boolean)
4 rules have insufficient validation logic
1 rule checks wrong field/concept (FileSystemProtection for snapshots)

test_results.note: Execution successful with no errors, but all 10 checks failed. Only 10 checks found when there should be 11 - one rule may not have executed (likely no resources found for that discovery). This indicates logic issues (value types, insufficient validation) rather than structural errors.

issues: Insufficient validation - only checks if KmsKeyId exists, doesn't verify KMS key policy follows least privilege
Value type verification needed for Encrypted field
aws.efs.filesystem.kms_key_policy_least_.recommendation: Enhance validation to check actual KMS key policy, verify value types

issues: Duplicate of resource.backup_enabled
Duplicate of have.backup_enabled
Wrong field/concept - FileSystemProtection is about deletion protection, not snapshots
EFS doesn't have traditional snapshots - uses backup policies instead
Should check backup policy instead of FileSystemProtection
aws.efs.filesystem.snapshots_enabled.recommendation: Change to check backup policy, or remove if snapshots don't apply to EFS

issues: Wrong validation - checks NumberOfMountTargets >= 1, but that doesn't verify Multi-AZ
A file system can have 1 mount target in a single AZ
Should check that mount targets exist in multiple availability zones
aws.efs.resource.multi_az_enabled.recommendation: Enhance validation to check mount targets are in multiple AZs

issues: Insufficient validation - only checks if SubnetId and VpcId exist, doesn't verify subnet is private
Mount targets always have SubnetId and VpcId
Duplicate of not_publicly_accessible.not_publicly_accessible_configured
Should check subnet configuration (e.g., mapPublicIpOnLaunch=false) or verify subnet is in private range
aws.efs.filesystem.private_network_only_.recommendation: Enhance validation to check subnet is actually private, consolidate duplicate

issues: Duplicate of filesystem.private_network_only_configured
Insufficient validation - same issues as rule #9
